resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows sync/sync.controller.ts but Step 8 CLI command uses --no-controller for sync module
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT create sync/sync.controller.ts in Epic 1. Only create sync.module.ts and sync.service.ts.
resolutions.reason: CLI command is the executable spec. Sync API endpoints belong to Epic 2. The sync module in Epic 1 is infrastructure only.

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows common/api-response.interface.ts but ApiResponse<T> already exists in shared/interfaces/index.ts
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT create common/api-response.interface.ts. Import ApiResponse<T> from @shared/interfaces in the interceptor.
resolutions.reason: Duplicating the interface creates drift risk. shared/interfaces/ exists for cross-layer type sharing.

resolutions.contradiction: Epic Step 5 says to possibly exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json. TypeScript needs to resolve @shared/* types for compilation. These are type-only imports and do not produce output in dist/.
resolutions.reason: Excluding ../shared would prevent tsc from resolving @shared/* imports at compile time, causing build failures.

resolutions.contradiction: Entity glob pattern __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}' appears to go up from config/ — is this correct?
resolutions.resolution: The glob is correct as written. At runtime, typeorm.config.ts is compiled to dist/config/typeorm.config.js, so __dirname is dist/config/. Going ../ reaches dist/. The glob dist/**/*.entity.js matches all compiled entities.
resolutions.reason: Traced compilation path: src/config/ compiles to dist/config/, __dirname = dist/config/, ../ = dist/, glob matches dist/agencies/agency.entity.js etc.

resolutions.contradiction: data/ directory does not exist and is not tracked by git; better-sqlite3 will fail if parent directory is missing
resolutions.resolution: The TypeORM config factory must call mkdirSync('data', { recursive: true }) before returning the SQLite config object.
resolutions.reason: better-sqlite3 does not create parent directories. The config factory is the correct place to ensure the directory exists before the database file is created.

resolutions.contradiction: GeoSpendingSnapshot unique constraint (stateCode, fiscalYear, agencyId, scope) includes nullable agencyId; SQLite treats NULLs as distinct in UNIQUE constraints, allowing duplicate rows with NULL agencyId
resolutions.resolution: Use the standard @Unique decorator as-is. Accept that SQLite allows multiple NULL agencyId rows with the same (stateCode, fiscalYear, scope). Deduplication is enforced at application level in Epic 2's sync upsert logic.
resolutions.reason: TypeORM does not natively support partial unique indexes. Using a sentinel value would change the data model's semantic contract. Application-level enforcement is standard practice for nullable unique columns in SQLite.

resolutions.contradiction: perCapita fields (GeoSpendingSnapshot, DisasterFundingRecord) are monetary-derived; unclear if stored as cents/person integer or dollars/person float
resolutions.resolution: Store perCapita as integer (cents per person), consistent with the 'all money values are integers' rule. Frontend divides by 100 for display.
resolutions.reason: perCapita = obligatedAmount(cents) / population. Storing as integer cents/person avoids floating-point issues and maintains consistency with the all-money-is-cents convention.

resolutions.contradiction: recoveryRatio (DisasterRecoveryRatio) is a dimensionless ratio (fedSpending/fema), not a monetary amount — should it be integer or float?
resolutions.resolution: Store recoveryRatio as float/decimal type. It is a ratio, not a monetary value. The 'money as cents integer' rule does not apply.
resolutions.reason: recoveryRatio = fedSpendingObligated / femaObligated produces values like 2.5. Forcing to integer would lose precision on a non-monetary derived field.

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows empty dto/ directories but git does not track empty directories
resolutions.resolution: Create each dto/ directory with a .gitkeep file so git tracks the directory. These are placeholders for Epic 3 DTOs.
resolutions.reason: File tree explicitly shows these directories. Creating them now prevents 'directory not found' issues when Epic 3 adds DTO files.

stories.title: Scaffold NestJS project and install all dependencies
stories.goal: Create the backend/ NestJS application with all required packages installed and .env.example

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend/package.json exists
backend/.env.example contains DATABASE_URL, FRONTEND_URL, and NODE_ENV
acceptanceCriteria.check: grep -q 'DATABASE_URL' backend/.env.example && grep -q 'FRONTEND_URL' backend/.env.example && grep -q 'NODE_ENV' backend/.env.example

acceptanceCriteria.text: All 8 runtime dependencies are in package.json: @nestjs/typeorm, typeorm, better-sqlite3, pg, @nestjs/config, class-validator, class-transformer, @nestjs/schedule
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); const deps=['@nestjs/typeorm','typeorm','better-sqlite3','pg','@nestjs/config','class-validator','class-transformer','@nestjs/schedule']; const missing=deps.filter(d=>!p.dependencies[d]); if(missing.length){console.error('Missing:',missing);process.exit(1)}"

acceptanceCriteria.text: @types/better-sqlite3 is in devDependencies
Default AppController and AppService are removed (no app.controller.ts or app.service.ts in src/)
pnpm build exits 0 from backend/

stories.notes: Run from repo root: npx @nestjs/cli new backend --package-manager pnpm --skip-git --strict. Then cd backend/ and run: pnpm add @nestjs/typeorm typeorm better-sqlite3 pg @nestjs/config class-validator class-transformer @nestjs/schedule && pnpm add -D @types/better-sqlite3. The pnpm workspace at root already references backend/ — this is expected and works with nest new. After scaffolding, DELETE the default app.controller.ts, app.controller.spec.ts, and app.service.ts created by the CLI, and remove their references from app.module.ts. Create .env.example with DATABASE_URL= (empty), FRONTEND_URL=http://localhost:4200, NODE_ENV=development.

stories.title: Configure @shared/* path alias in tsconfig
stories.goal: Wire up the @shared/* path alias so backend code can import from shared/interfaces

acceptanceCriteria.text: tsconfig.json compilerOptions contains paths with @shared/* mapping to ../shared/*
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && node -e "const ts=require('./tsconfig.json'); const paths=ts.compilerOptions.paths; if(!paths||!paths['@shared/*']||!paths['@shared/*'].includes('../shared/*')){console.error('Missing @shared/* path alias');process.exit(1)}"

acceptanceCriteria.text: tsconfig.build.json does NOT exclude ../shared
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && node -e "const ts=require('./tsconfig.build.json'); if(ts.exclude && ts.exclude.some(e=>e.includes('shared'))){console.error('shared should not be excluded');process.exit(1)}"

acceptanceCriteria.text: pnpm build exits 0 with the path alias configured

stories.notes: Add '"paths": { "@shared/*": ["../shared/*"] }' to compilerOptions in tsconfig.json. Do NOT exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json (see resolutions). The alias is resolved at compile time by tsc and at runtime by tsconfig-paths (used by nest start). No changes to nest-cli.json should be needed for path alias support.

stories.title: Configure TypeORM dual-database (SQLite / Postgres) and wire into AppModule
stories.goal: Set up TypeORM with environment-based switching between SQLite (dev) and Postgres (prod), registered via ConfigModule

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend/src/config/typeorm.config.ts exists
Exports a getTypeOrmConfig function that accepts ConfigService
Contains better-sqlite3 branch (type: 'better-sqlite3')
Contains postgres branch (type: 'postgres') triggered by DATABASE_URL env var
Config creates data/ directory via mkdirSync before SQLite connection
SQLite branch uses synchronize: true, Postgres branch uses synchronize: false, migrationsRun: true
Entity glob pattern uses __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}'
AppModule imports ConfigModule.forRoot with isGlobal: true
AppModule imports TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync using getTypeOrmConfig
pnpm build exits 0

stories.notes: The config factory MUST call mkdirSync('data', { recursive: true }) before returning the SQLite config — better-sqlite3 will fail if the parent directory doesn't exist. The path 'data/' is relative to the backend/ working directory (where nest start runs from). The entity glob pattern __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}' is CORRECT as written (see resolutions). AppModule must import ConfigModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true }) BEFORE TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync, since the config factory injects ConfigService.

stories.title: Create 6 NestJS modules with controller/service scaffolds
stories.goal: Generate all 6 modules with correct controllers and services, register in AppModule, create dto/ directories

acceptanceCriteria.text: All 6 module files exist
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in agencies spending geography disaster sync health; do test -f backend/src/$m/$m.module.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: agencies, spending, geography, disaster each have a controller
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in agencies spending geography disaster; do test -f backend/src/$m/$m.controller.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: agencies, spending, geography, disaster, sync each have a service
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in agencies spending geography disaster sync; do test -f backend/src/$m/$m.service.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: sync module does NOT have a controller file
health module does NOT have a service file
dto/ directories exist with .gitkeep for agencies, spending, geography, disaster
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in agencies spending geography disaster; do test -f backend/src/$m/dto/.gitkeep || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: All 6 modules are registered in AppModule imports
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in AgenciesModule SpendingModule GeographyModule DisasterModule SyncModule HealthModule; do grep -q "$m" backend/src/app.module.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: pnpm build exits 0

stories.notes: Use NestJS CLI commands (npx nest g module/controller/service <name>) or write files manually. IMPORTANT: sync module gets module + service ONLY, no controller (contradiction resolved — see resolutions). Health module gets module + controller ONLY, no service (controller injects DataSource directly for DB check). If using CLI commands, the CLI auto-registers modules in AppModule — verify S03's TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync is preserved. After generating, remove any *.spec.ts test files the CLI creates. Create dto/.gitkeep files with: mkdir -p backend/src/<module>/dto && touch backend/src/<module>/dto/.gitkeep.

stories.title: Define all 5 TypeORM entities with columns, types, relations, and unique constraints
stories.goal: Create all 5 entity classes matching data-model.md exactly, with integer monetary columns, FK relations, and composite unique constraints

acceptanceCriteria.text: All 5 entity files exist
acceptanceCriteria.check: for f in agencies/agency.entity.ts spending/spending-record.entity.ts geography/geo-spending-snapshot.entity.ts disaster/disaster-funding-record.entity.ts disaster/disaster-recovery-ratio.entity.ts; do test -f backend/src/$f || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: Each entity file has @Entity decorator
acceptanceCriteria.check: for f in agencies/agency.entity.ts spending/spending-record.entity.ts geography/geo-spending-snapshot.entity.ts disaster/disaster-funding-record.entity.ts disaster/disaster-recovery-ratio.entity.ts; do grep -q '@Entity' backend/src/$f || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: Agency entity has columns: id (PK auto-increment), name, abbreviation, toptierCode
SpendingRecord has @Unique constraint on (agencyId, fiscalYear, quarter, awardTypeLabel)
GeoSpendingSnapshot has @Unique constraint on (stateCode, fiscalYear, agencyId, scope)
DisasterFundingRecord has @Unique constraint on (defGroup, stateCode)
SpendingRecord.agencyId is a ManyToOne relation to Agency
GeoSpendingSnapshot.agencyId is a nullable ManyToOne relation to Agency
Monetary columns (obligatedAmount, outlayAmount, femaObligated, fedSpendingObligated) use integer type
recoveryRatio in DisasterRecoveryRatio uses float/decimal type (NOT integer)
Each module with entities registers them via TypeOrmModule.forFeature
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in agencies spending geography disaster; do grep -q 'TypeOrmModule.forFeature' backend/src/$m/$m.module.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: pnpm build exits 0

stories.notes: CRITICAL TYPE RULES: (1) All monetary amount fields (obligatedAmount, outlayAmount, femaObligated, fedSpendingObligated) MUST be integer columns — these store cents, never floats. (2) perCapita fields (GeoSpendingSnapshot, DisasterFundingRecord) are integer — cents per person. (3) recoveryRatio (DisasterRecoveryRatio) is float/decimal — it is a dimensionless ratio, NOT money. (4) Count fields (awardCount, population, declarationCount) are integer. Use TypeORM @ManyToOne for FK relations, not raw @Column. GeoSpendingSnapshot.agencyId must be nullable. The @Unique decorator uses array syntax: @Unique('UQ_name', ['col1', 'col2', ...]). See resolution on SQLite NULL behavior in unique constraints. DisasterRecoveryRatio has NO unique constraint (none specified in data model).

stories.title: Response wrapper interceptor and @Paginated() decorator
stories.goal: Create the common response wrapper infrastructure with @Paginated() decorator for paginated list endpoints

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend/src/common/response-wrapper.interceptor.ts exists
backend/src/common/paginated.decorator.ts exists
Interceptor imports ApiResponse from @shared/interfaces (NOT a local duplicate)
Interceptor class implements NestInterceptor
Decorator file exports a @Paginated() decorator function
Interceptor checks for @Paginated() metadata to decide whether to wrap
Interceptor is registered as APP_INTERCEPTOR global provider in AppModule
common/api-response.interface.ts does NOT exist (no duplicate)
pnpm build exits 0

stories.notes: DO NOT create common/api-response.interface.ts — ApiResponse<T> already exists in shared/interfaces/index.ts (see resolutions). The @Paginated() decorator uses SetMetadata to mark controller methods. The interceptor uses Reflector to read that metadata. When metadata is present, wrap response as { data: T[], meta: { total, page, pageSize } }. When absent, return T directly (no wrapping). The interceptor should handle both cases. Register as { provide: APP_INTERCEPTOR, useClass: ResponseWrapperInterceptor } in AppModule providers. The interceptor does NOT need to extract total/page/pageSize from the response itself at this stage — that wiring happens in Epic 3 when paginated endpoints are implemented. For now the interceptor structure must be correct and build cleanly.

stories.title: CORS, global validation pipe, and GET /health endpoint
stories.goal: Wire up CORS via FRONTEND_URL env var, enable global ValidationPipe, and implement GET /health that verifies database connectivity

acceptanceCriteria.text: main.ts enables CORS using FRONTEND_URL env var with fallback to '*'
main.ts registers global ValidationPipe with whitelist: true and transform: true
acceptanceCriteria.check: grep -q 'ValidationPipe' backend/src/main.ts && grep -q 'whitelist' backend/src/main.ts && grep -q 'transform' backend/src/main.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: HealthController has a GET /health endpoint
HealthController returns object with status, timestamp, and database fields
acceptanceCriteria.check: grep -q 'status' backend/src/health/health.controller.ts && grep -q 'timestamp' backend/src/health/health.controller.ts && grep -q 'database' backend/src/health/health.controller.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: HealthController injects DataSource to verify DB connectivity
HealthModule does not import TypeOrmModule (DataSource is globally available via forRootAsync)
pnpm build exits 0

stories.notes: CORS setup: app.enableCors({ origin: process.env.FRONTEND_URL || '*' }). ValidationPipe: app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true, transform: true })). Health endpoint: inject DataSource from typeorm, check dataSource.isInitialized or run dataSource.query('SELECT 1'). Return { status: 'ok', timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), database: 'connected' }. If DB check fails, return { status: 'error', timestamp: ..., database: 'disconnected' } with appropriate HTTP status (503). The health module does NOT need to import TypeOrmModule since TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync in AppModule makes DataSource globally available for injection. Do NOT use @nestjs/terminus — keep it simple as specified in the epic.

stories.title: End-to-end verification of Epic 1
stories.goal: Start the full backend and verify all epic acceptance criteria pass

acceptanceCriteria.text: pnpm start:dev starts without errors (process runs for 15+ seconds without crash)
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && rm -f data/fedspend.sqlite && (pnpm start:dev > /tmp/nest-epic1.log 2>&1 &) && for i in $(seq 1 30); do sleep 1 && curl -sf http://localhost:3000/health > /dev/null 2>&1 && break; done && curl -sf http://localhost:3000/health > /dev/null; EXIT=$?; kill $(lsof -t -i:3000) 2>/dev/null; exit $EXIT

acceptanceCriteria.text: GET /health returns JSON with status 'ok' and database 'connected'
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && (pnpm start:dev > /tmp/nest-epic1.log 2>&1 &) && for i in $(seq 1 30); do sleep 1 && curl -sf http://localhost:3000/health > /dev/null 2>&1 && break; done && RESP=$(curl -sf http://localhost:3000/health); kill $(lsof -t -i:3000) 2>/dev/null; echo "$RESP" | grep -q '"ok"' && echo "$RESP" | grep -q '"connected"'

acceptanceCriteria.text: data/fedspend.sqlite file is created after server start
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && (pnpm start:dev > /tmp/nest-epic1.log 2>&1 &) && for i in $(seq 1 30); do sleep 1 && test -f data/fedspend.sqlite && break; done; kill $(lsof -t -i:3000) 2>/dev/null; test -f data/fedspend.sqlite

acceptanceCriteria.text: SQLite file contains all 5 entity tables
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && node -e "const db=require('better-sqlite3')('data/fedspend.sqlite'); const tables=db.prepare(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name NOT LIKE 'sqlite_%'\").all().map(r=>r.name); db.close(); if(tables.length<5){console.error('Expected 5+ tables, found:',tables);process.exit(1)} console.log('Tables:',tables.sort().join(', '))"

acceptanceCriteria.text: All 6 modules are registered in AppModule
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in AgenciesModule SpendingModule GeographyModule DisasterModule SyncModule HealthModule; do grep -q "$m" backend/src/app.module.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: TypeORM config switches between SQLite and Postgres based on DATABASE_URL
acceptanceCriteria.check: grep -q 'better-sqlite3' backend/src/config/typeorm.config.ts && grep -q 'postgres' backend/src/config/typeorm.config.ts && grep -q 'DATABASE_URL' backend/src/config/typeorm.config.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: Response wrapper interceptor and @Paginated decorator exist
CORS is configured via FRONTEND_URL in main.ts
No frontend code exists in backend/
shared/interfaces/index.ts is importable from backend via @shared/* alias (verify interceptor imports it)

stories.notes: This is a VERIFICATION-ONLY story. Do NOT create or modify any files. If any criterion fails, the issue belongs to the story that owns the failing file. This story confirms the entire epic is complete. The server start checks use a background process with curl polling — the server is started, health is checked, then the server is killed. Ensure port 3000 is free before running. Clean up data/fedspend.sqlite before the first start to verify it is created fresh.
